title: pittosporum huttonianum
intro: Pittosporum huttonianum, also known as the 'Kawakawa' or 'New Zealand Pepper Tree', is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Pittosporaceae. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in Māori medicine, valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pittosporum huttonianum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Pittosporum huttonianum is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and also used in traditional Māori ceremonies.
